title: storage — subscribed events
description: Cross-module reactions this module handles. Internal reactions (own events consumed by own listeners) are documented in events.json under `consumers`.

listeners.purpose: Discover every #[FileKind] class + #[Attachable] property across every module. Compile into FileKindRegistry (Redis-backed) so runtime enforcement is a hash-map lookup.

Fires INSIDE the tenant-provisioning transaction. Reserves the tenant's storage disk assignment (S3 region + bucket path prefix). Does not upload anything — just sets the routing metadata so subsequent uploads know where to land.

Cascade-erase every File belonging to the erased tenant. Runs as a batched job because a tenant can have many thousands of files. Decrements each blob's refcount (may leave shared blobs untouched when other tenants still reference them). Emits one FileErased per File with erasure_reason=tenant_erased.

When a user is deleted, their owner_id becomes null on every File they uploaded (Postgres FK onDelete=set null). This listener writes an audit trail entry per orphaned file + emits FileArchived on files with `#[Attachable(on_replace: 'archive')]` where the user was also the fileable owner.
Security response: on password change, revoke every active signed URL user_lock'd to that user's ULID. Prevents a compromised-and-shared link from surviving a password rotation.

When compliance module fires an erasure DSAR for a subject, iterate every File where the subject appears in owner_id, issued_to_user_id (via signed URLs), or metadata references. Emit FileErased with erasure_reason=dsar_erasure. Compliance module reads audit trail to build the DSAR completion report.

Aggregate the metadata of every File referencing the subject into the DSAR export ZIP. Physical byte export requires an additional tenant-admin approval + is handled by a separate ExportFileBytesForSubject action.
On plan tier change, update the tenant's signed-URL TTL caps + max_per_hour rate limits. Existing signed URLs are not retroactively revoked.
